entry.title: Re: Material Quantity Unit Conversion using CDS Views
entry.content:
Is there a way of combining the T006 and the MARM approaches? 

I am trying to get equivalent unit prices of a material from a purchasing info record.  

For example, I have a material which has the unit conversion:
1 EA <=> 75 KG

and my purchasing info record condition has the price as
75 EUR per 1 TO

and I need to find out what the EUR value is per EA.

My issue here is I have one MARM entry and one T006 entry, but I don't know beforehand what I'm going to encounter.  I am currenly using a virtual element and calling function module MD_CONVERT_MATERIAL_UNIT to go from TO to EA in the above example, but this causes other problems in that I can't compare the resulting value with another field as it's the last thing that gets calculated.

Has anyone come up with a pure CDS answer to this?

Many thanks,

Andrew

entry.title: Re: UI5 UploadSet beforeItemAdded event no longer fires.
entry.content: @WouterLemaire, I can't figure out how to answer your question specifically. But my answer is: I appreciate the help and any further help. This is the info I get if I do a 'ui5 -v' in the cli: @ui5/cli: 3.9.0 @ui5/builder: 3.3.0 @ui5/server: 3.1.5 @ui5/fs: 3.0.5 @ui5/project: 3.9.0 @ui5/logger: 3.0.0

entry.title: Re: Calculate share in SAC on total amount for article
entry.content:
Hi Maria,

you may try the following in the BW query

  • Let A be dimension Article
  • Let CPG be dimension Customer Pricing Group
  • Let S be measure (key figure) Sales

The query might have A, CPG drilled-down in the rows. Create an RKF (restricted key figure) as follows:

  • RKF is based on S
  • Add CPG as restriction in the RKF and using the context menu mark this dimension as 'Constant Selection'

Create a formula F (% Share of Sale with const. CPG) as follows:

  • F:= S / RKF * 100

This query should return the values you want.

Regards,

Gregor

entry.title: New member introduction
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,
 
My name is Shanika Packard, and I’m excited to be a part of this SAP Learning Community! A
little about myself: I’m an experienced crisis response and mental health professional with a
passion for helping individuals navigate challenging situations. Over the years, I’ve worked in
various roles where I’ve developed strategies for crisis management, coordinated humanitarian
efforts, and collaborated with law enforcement to ensure the safety and well-being of those in
need. Currently, I’m working as an Enrollment Counselor at my alma mater University of
Phoenix, where I help students enroll in degree programs and support their individual
personalized student plans. My background also includes experience as a Mental Health Case
Manager and Technician, where I focused on providing care, managing treatment plans, and
leading therapeutic groups. I’m now pursuing my Master’s in Clinical Mental Health Counseling
to further my ability to support others and make a positive impact in the mental health field
 
I’m currently working towards my SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP
S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with the Vower learning group, and I’m really looking forward to
expanding my knowledge in Healthcare and\or Pharmaceuticals
 
I’m particularly excited about obtaining this certification because it opens doors for significant
career growth and positions me at the forefront of digital transformation. Also, this certification
equips me with the tools to help healthcare organizations implement streamlined processes,
improve efficiency, and stay competitive in today’s fast-paced business environment. Plus, it
aligns perfectly with my desire to continue to work in the healthcare industry.
 
I’m also open to connecting with others who are interested in healthcare, particularly in how
technology and business transformation can improve patient care, operational efficiency, and
regulatory compliance. I’m eager to explore how SAP S/4HANA can be leveraged in the
healthcare industry to drive innovation and streamline processes.
Looking forward to learning and growing with all of you!
 
Best regards,  
Shanika Packard

entry.title: Re: BODS Connectivity with Anaplan Data Hub
entry.content: Yes, you can use BODS (BusinessObjects Data Services) to extract and load data directly to Anaplan Data Hub using Anaplan APIs without needing additional integrators. Just ensure BODS is configured to handle API calls appropriately.

entry.title: Re: Embrace Defect Corrections with SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content: Thank you for this insightful post! I’m curious about the timeline for upcoming features that could streamline the process in SAP Cloud ALM even further. Are there any details available about the roadmap or expected release dates for these enhancements? Looking forward to any updates you can share!

Hi @harivenkatesh_s ,

I'm also unable to play the videos. I'm asked to log in to watch the video, but do not have access - see error message below. I think only SAP employees can view them. Can you please host them somewhere publicly available?

Thanks,

Hugh

 

Error message

"AADSTS90072: User account 'XXX' from identity provider 'XXX' does not exist in tenant 'SAP SE' and cannot access the application 'https://accounts.sap.com'(SAP SE: SAPID UnifiedAuthentication PROD) in that tenant. The account needs to be added as an external user in the tenant first. Sign out and sign in again with a different Azure Active Directory user account"

entry.title: Re: DS 4.1 SAP Material - Add leading Zeros - How?

entry.title: Displaying Equipment Details on Service Map / FSM
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I’m trying to display equipment details on the service map, similar to how activity and technician objects are displayed. I attempted to achieve this through screen configuration, but it didn’t work as expected.

I customized the screen CSMapEquipmentDetail based on the standard screen CSMapEquipmentDetail-[screenconfigurator] and activated it, but no changes were reflected on the service map.

Did I miss an additional configuration step? Could anyone please provide some guidance or insights on this issue?

Thank you in advance for your help.

entry.title: Re: i had installed the vs code but when i try to access the template wizard blow error was displaye
entry.content:
Application Wizard is not currently compatible with VSCode 1.94.x released in recent days. 
Please temporarily install 1.93 of VSCode instead from https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/supporting/faq#_previous-release-versions 

FYI https://github.com/SAP/yeoman-ui/pull/853

entry.title: Re: how to download SAP GUI 8.0 for MAC OS, I am P user,...
entry.content: Please look into: https://developers.sap.com/trials-downloads.html?search=SAP+GUI+for+Java

entry.title: Re: Task type in parallel processing
entry.content: There are no differences between dialog and background workprocesses, you may arrange how many workprocesses are assigned to DIA or BTC. That won't block users. You indicate the ratio of dialog workprocesses reserved to users (check the profile parameters). RFC runs in dialog tasks because they should be sized to run small units of work (like a dialog operation). bgRFC also runs via dialog workprocesses but you may select 3 priorities with each a different timeout (profile parameters again).

entry.title: Re: Calculated Inspection Characteristics using BAPI_INSP...
entry.content:
Dear mouradche,

thank you for the details provided.

If you have the input characteristics closed for the formula, you need to run the BAPI for the calculated characteristics separately. This part you have identified properly.

I have the feeling you try to close the calculated characteristic also on SINGLE_RESULTS level, however, it should be triggered on characteristic level, that is table CHAR_RESULTS of the BAPI.

Please re-test with help of the guidance above.

I wish you a nice day,
Albert
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: how to download SAP GUI 8.0 for MAC OS, I am P user,...
entry.content:
As a freelancer, you can’t obtain an S-user ID. You might consider using SAP's trial versions or contacting SAP directly for alternative access options.
you can check out the SAP Developer Center for trial versions and resources: SAP Developer Center. For support, you can visit: SAP Support Portal

entry.title: No me aparece en ningún documento mi precio guardado en DMA.
entry.content: Al entrar a datos maestros del artículo, le pongo precio a uno en particular, pero al hacer una orden de venta del mismo no me estira el precio guardado previamente. Pasa lo mismo cuando creo un descuento específico por cliente, el mismo no me estira. Queda en cero al crear nuevos documentos.

entry.title: Re: Error message "No space left on device" during build of CAP application

entry.title: Re: SAP RAP Unmanaged Lock(No Draft)
entry.content: @Andre_Fischer But that would only lock the table entry within the newly created odata service by checking the draft table, the database table entry would still be modifiable by other odata services or transactions, wouldn´t it?

entry.title: Re: Inspection lot number range status doesn't change
entry.content:
Dear fatima_mukhtar,

thank you for the details provided. 

I agree with the peers who have answered earlier, it seems to be a buffering issue.

Please read this KBA in the topic:

1832772 - Quality inspection lot number skip

Furthermore, about the buffering and buffer reset read - for example - this SAP documentation:

'Administration of the Number Range Buffer'

https://searchtest.hana.ondemand.com/docs/ABAP_PLATFORM_NEW/4a09ec86347e48e894861231ad159115/47d565a3167e3c84e10000000a42189c.html?locale=en-US

I wish you a nice day,

Albert
SAP Support

entry.title: Enhancement of Payment Formats in Contract Accounting
entry.content:
When you send a payment file to the bank but find out that your bank has specific requirements regarding to file layouts, then you need to modify the layouts accordingly. This blog post introduces how you can adjust mapping structures, or file layouts, of the payment formats with Business Add-Ins (BAdIs) in Cloud.

If you use S/4HANA instead of SAP S/4HANA Cloud, you can read the blog post Enhancement Implementation in Payment Formats in Contract Accounts Receivable and Payable (FI-CA).

To learn more about payment formats in Contract Accounting, you can refer to the blog post Payment Formats in Contract Accounting in SAP S/4HANA.

Payment process in Contract Accounting

 

The new feature enables you to create your own mapping structure to your own payment format and you can further implement the logic via Business Add-ins (BAdIs) in Cloud.

To complete this task for processing payment, you need to complete the tasks as follows:

Procedure in red for setting up payment medium formats. After completing these three activities, you assigned them to Payment method as standard procedure.

  1. Map format data for payments
  2. Define payment medium formats
  3. Implement Cloud BAdI (optional)

Procedure in yellow for generating payment medium files:

  1. Define Variants for Payment Medium Program
  2. Define Variant Determination for Payment Medium Program   

Map Format Data for Payments

You can modify the standard mapping structure that is delivered by SAP in the activity Map Format Data for Payments.

  1. In your configuration environment, open Map Format Data for Payments (ID: 100558)
  2. Take Canada as an example, you find one format mapping name from the list and select Copy.
    In general, the customers’ structure starts with a slash “/”.
  3. After you copied the standard mapping structure, you find the status being “Maintenance”. Select the arrow on the right to adjust the mapping or add values you require.
  4. Edit.
  5. For example, you can add a new element in the structure.
  6. Select Release, when you finish editing. The status thus changed from “maintenance” to “released”.

Define Payment Medium Formats

After defining your own mapping structure, you assign it to your own payment medium formats. You can copy standard payment medium formats that are delivered by SAP and modify them based on your business requirements.

Two new fields are introduced to this configuration step:

  • Original Payment Medium Format – You must enter an original payment format so the essential values delivered by SAP can be copied to your payment formats.
  • Deviating Format Tree – You can select the mapping structure that you created in the previous configuration step.

To create your own payment formats, follow the steps below:

  1. In your configuration environment open Define Payment Medium Formats (ID: 106730).
  2. Select one standard payment medium format and select Copy As.
  3. When copying the payment format, you can enter the name of payment format starting with a slash “/”. Otherwise, after copying as a new format, the system automatically adds up a slash.

    Select “copy all” in the pop-up screen to copy the whole payment medium structure. Your newly created payment medium format appears on the list.

(Optional) Implement Cloud BAdI

As a key user, optionally you can use the cloud business add-in (BAdI) to implement your own logic. Before using the cloud BAdI, ensure that you have assigned the business role SAP_BR_EXTENSIBILITY_SPEC.

Note: Regarding of extensibility options for developers, go to Business Accelerator Hub and search for Adjust Payment Data (for Customer).

  1. Open Custom Logic app.
  2. Select “Create”.
  3. Enter the extension point BADI_FKK_ADJUST_PAYM_DATA and search.
    In step 2 implementation attributes, select Add.
  4. Select “Add” in the pop-up window to apply filter condition.
    Enter the payment medium format that you created in the value field.

    After you save the condition, the filter with the format is visible.

  5. Proceed to the third step and enter the implementation description and ID. Select “Review”.
  6. Select “Create” button.
  7. Select the “Publish” button, the status changes from Created to Published.
  8. You can then Open Code Editor.
    You can select Show Sample Code and copy them to clipboard to better change the coding based on your business needs.

Define Variants for Payment Medium Program

This activity allows you to define a variant for each payment medium format in the payment run.

  1. In your configuration environment open Define Variants for Payment Medium Program (ID: 101276).
  2. Enter the Format Short Name that you created.
  3. Input the fields for the payment runs of your payment medium formats.
    Note: Fields can vary based on the selected payment medium formats.
  4. Save your entry.

Define Variant Determination for Payment Medium Program

This activity enables you to set up the account determination, such as Paying Company Code, House Bank Account, Account ID, Payment Method to the payment format variants that you have prepared in previous activity.

  1. In your configuration environment open Define variant determination for payment medium program (ID: 102207).
  2. Create a new entry and enter your format name. Select continue.
  3. Input fields in the Key section and name your Variant.
  4. Save your entry.

Is this blog post useful to you? Please leave your comments or questions below.

entry.title: From Parallel Accounting to Universal Parallel Accounting and a focus on Parallel valuation
entry.content:
“Parallel Acounting” isn’t a new term at all (779251 - mySAP ERP New general ledger: Parallel accounting - SAP for Me). We have delivered it with two approaches – Accounts approach and Ledger approach. With SAP S/4HANA, we recommend the “ledger approach”. But we also understand that there are some restrictions – especially when it comes to “management accounting”. This is one of the drivers behind continuously innovating and improving our solution and coming up with “Universal Parallel Accounting” (UPA). (Our colleague Sarah has written an excellent blog which I would like to refer here: Universal Parallel Accounting in SAP S/4HANA - SAP Community)

Let’s look at some of our customer requirements and see how we have addressed those in a pre-UPA world:

One of our affiliates could be based out of India and India reports from April to March, whereas the HQ in Germany reports from January to December. This means we have a shifted fiscal year for one of our affiliates, so a common management accounting model wasn’t possible. We did have a work around and created a separate “controlling area” for India.

Another one of our affiliates is based out of Brazil and in Brazil, we valuate the inventory at actual costs. So far, we have always valuated inventory based on the group accounting standards, since the standard cost was not ledger specific. We were not able to calculate an actual cost based on local GAAP requirements.

Next we come to currency requirements, we could be based in a country which has a company code currency and it reports to a company headquartered in Europe, so a different global currency. But we could also be required to follow all accounting transactions in an additional currency, a so called “functional currency”. (Kindly refer to the note for more information on functional currencies https://me.sap.com/notes/3200089/E)

And finally, our supply chains are global and there are value flows from one country to another as we add value to our products. So, we need a clear picture on the valuation from a legal and group perspective, and this was always possible with our transfer pricing solution, however it has also been simplified and improved with UPA.

Universal Parallel Accounting is our answer to valuate business processes in parallel and in real time, for each accounting principle for legal and group consolidation purposes.

Whenever I do my pitch on SAP S/4HANA, I always start with a value driver tree, and I focus on one part – if I am talking about the P&L, then I will run through the different lines in the P&L and show how SAP S/4HANA adds value compared to ECC. And UPA touches every part of our financial value driver tree.

Let us now look at one of the use cases delivered with UPA: Parallel Accounting from a Consolidated Perspective

Let’s say that I am working in a manufacturing company with operations spread across multiple countries. For economies of scale, let us assume that I do the first step of my production process in one of my European plants, for example Germany and then transfer it to my US plant for delayed differentiation.

For this, I would need to calculate a standard cost from a legal perspective, and I would also like to calculate a standard cost at a group level without the intercompany margins from the perspective of a group controller.

Previously it was possible to store three different valuations, in two currencies in the material master, however, it wasn’t possible to calculate a standard cost estimate for each accounting principle. This is one of the key innovations with Universal Parallel Accounting (UPA). When we talk about parallel valuation, we can calculate a ledger specific pricing – both at a legal level and as well as a group level

Before UPA, we had given two possibilities for customers in the context of parallel valuation:

  • Multiple ledgers, single valuation
  • Single ledger, multiple valuation

But we had always recommended the use of single ledger, multiple valuation approach due to certain restrictions in Asset Accounting.

With Universal Parallel Accounting (UPA), we have linked the valuation to the ledger and we can calculate a standard cost for each ledger. This also means that the currency and valuation profile becomes obsolete with UPA and the ledger is the leading object for which we maintain the currency settings.

In addition to this, from a costing perspective, we have also delivered additional costing variants (P00L, P02L, P03L) which can be used to calculate a ledger specific cost estimate, or you could also choose to continue to use the costing variants such PPC1, PYC1 etc to update the same value into all ledgers, but the key change is that you need to mark and release the cost estimates separately for each ledger.

Let’s look at our example where one part of the processing happens in one legal entity and another part of the processing happens in a second legal entity.

In terms of logistics processes, our first production step takes place in Germany for Material ZPREETHA_FG_01. It goes through three production lines, consumes raw materials, semi-finished goods, etc. This material is transferred to a plant in USA. For the sake of simplicity, I have done a transfer with just an intercompany margin added to it. However, in reality, we could have some additional (landing) costs as we do the transfer. It then goes through additional processing and is then transformed to a new material code ZPREETHA_FG_02.

Let us look at this from a costing perspective:

In the first production step, the product is manufactured in plant 1010 in Germany. We can see the cost component split from both the legal and group perspective. Now when this product is transferred to USA, an intercompany margin is added at the plant 1710 in USA. However, from a group perspective, we still retain a margin-free valuation and in addition, we also have the detail of the cost component split. In the next production step, the product ZPREETHA_FG_01 undergoes further processing and value addition to transform to product ZPREETHA_FG_02.

From the legal perspective, we have purchased the product ZPREETHA_FG_01 at 37,14 USD/PC (which includes an intercompany margin) and added additional value to it. However, from the group perspective, we still see the margin free values, even though our product has gone through a value add and changed material codes, as well as the original cost component split which would give a controller at a group level all necessary information to calculate a MVC.

This is how group costing caters to the requirements of a controller at the group level who doesn’t lose the granularity despite the fact that the material has moved from one plant to another and has also changed material codes as value gets added progressively across the supply chain.

In my example, the legal entity 1010 from Germany sells to a customer in US for which the delivery is done by the affiliate in US (1710). There the intercompany purchase and sale across Germany and USA has additional intercompany margins added to the price of the product.

Some screenshots to do a quick recap of how a product looks like at the final selling entity perspective from both the legal perspective:

Legal Valuation of material ZPREETHA_FG_02 from Plant 1010 belonging to the legal entity 1010 based in Germany:

Group Valuation of material ZPREETHA_FG_02 from Plant 1010 belonging to the legal entity 1010 based in Germany:

The next step is a sale to customer. Let us assume that the company is Germany is selling directly to a customer in the US, however, the products are already in the US belonging to the US affiliate, so this US affiliate can deliver directly to the customer. Therefore, an intercompany sales/purchase flow comes into play. This is handled by the new Advanced intercompany processes which was released in 2022. I will also introduce the concept of transactional consolidation in Part 2 of my Blog as we analyze the accounting postings.

Before we consolidate our figures, we first need to match and reconcile this intercompany purchase and sale. I will use ICMR to show how our ‘in-built’ solution does the matching and reconciliation without any cumbersome Extract Transform Load (ETL) processes and how any latencies with respect to the intercompany processes are eliminated with this integrated approach. This will be detailed in Part 3 of my Blog.

entry.title: Re: Migrate the ASE database (On-Prem) to the SAP hana Cl...
entry.content: Hi Javier, Thanks for all the inputs. We are able to use the Extract_offline method to process the DDL files generated from the ASE database and proceed further on subsequent steps.

entry.title: Re: Asset Revaluation & Acquisition in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition via SAP Build Process Au
entry.content:
Hello Shadab.

SAP Build  include SAP Build Apps, SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Build Workzone that can help you create and augment enterprise apps, automate processes, and design business sites.

SAP Build Process Automation is typically hosted on the SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). By hosting SAP Build Process Automation on SAP BTP, you can take advantage of SAP's robust cloud infrastructure, comprehensive suite of services, and seamless integration capabilities to automate and optimize business processes effectively

SAP Build Process Automation is an SAP BTP service that allows you to create, run, automate, and monitor your business processes on one interface using low-code/no-code capabilities.

Architecture diagram gives an overview of all components of SAP Build Process Automation.

SAP BTP is a cloud-based platform, enabling organizations to build, manage, and run their applications in a cloud environment. This inherently supports scalability, reliability, and performance.

SAP Build can integrate seamlessly with other services available on SAP BTP, such as SAP Integration Suite, SAP Data Intelligence, and SAP Analytics Cloud.

Hope this helps.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi David,

          I am venkata jyothi 

  • Women
  • People in remote or underserved locations that may be not have access to the same economic opportunities as their urban or more privileged counterparts 
  • People looking to switch their careers mid-life
  • People who lack the financial means to afford a certification attempt for themselves   

Please provide campaign code

Thanks & Regards,

gogadajyothi.

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024
entry.content: Welcome to my coding corner!! 😀

entry.title: New
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

 

My name is Péréscar Germain, and I’m excited to be a part of this SAP Learning Community! A little about myself:  I've been working in the warehouse industry for almost years, and have an associate's degree in Design Media Production. 

I’m currently working towards my SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with the Vower learning group, and I’m looking forward to expanding my knowledge in this SAP cloud learning program.  

I’m particularly excited about obtaining this certification because it will expand my career options in a field that I'm interested in and from what I've been told will be expanding worldwide as a standard for a lot of companies. 

I’m also open to connecting with others interested in anyone in 3d animation, graphic design field, Ai integration.

I am looking forward to learning and growing with all of you!

Best regards,  

Péréscar Germain

entry.title: Re: Multiple entry option in Input parameters of Graphica...
entry.content:
Hi Martin,

Thanks for reply. I Used this filters option in analytical model works fine.(Here there was no option to point the variable to Master data objects to show key and text search help to user).

I wanted to use Input parameters to pass multiple values into my SQL Code where clause, to address some complex logic and improve performance, like we do in native HANA Table functions (Here in graphical/SQL view input parameters can be created referring to Master data objects to show key and text search help to user, same option is not there in analytical model filter variable.

 

Thanks,

Joe

entry.title: How to append an existing custom structure to a standard table
entry.content:
Hi,

I created a custom structure and I need to add it in append inside a standard table.

but if I click the "Append structure" button it tells me that the structure already exists:

I don't want to create the structure, because it already exists, but I just need to add it to the standard table. How can I do that?

 

thank you

entry.title: Re: SAP Material management Consultant level Certification
entry.content: https://help.sap.com/learning-journeys/50047c917a2610149575e3c6cab404af

entry.title: sap b1 minimum stock level
entry.content:
Hi

Looking for some help with managing minimum stock levels.

I have added minimum stock levels to items held in various warehouse. 

In the 'Bin Location Content List' report, or any query report, the minimum quantities are not displaying?

Do I have a setting wrong somewhere?

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: SAP Material management Consultant level Certification
entry.content:
Hi @Margit_Wagner,...

Thanks for your answer... I was trying to access to the Learning Journey SAP S/4HANA - Sourcing and Procurement without log in! So, this way, this page cannot be accessed.

🤗

entry.title: Re: ABAP to JSON
entry.content:
Thanks Tomas, this old answer pointed me in the right direction: the system I was working on indeed has such an old release that the standard /UI2/CL_JSON does not properly support camelCase and the Notes seem to be for much newer releases.

Fortunately, SAP provides a replacement, abap-to-json, which works beautifully!

entry.title: Re: Advanced Test Orchestration and Tester assignment using Test Plans in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hello Reiner,

I'm using test plans for e2e testing. The issue we have is how to get the test cases in sequence. fi. 

- TC 2

- TC 3

- TC 1.

You mentioned that Test Sequences, define the sequences of test cases within a test plan is planned as an enhancement. For e2e testing it is really a must to have.

When do you expect this enhancement will be available?

Have a nice day,

Hans

entry.title: SAP Hana error XXX: Number XXX not defined for material type
entry.content:
Hi there, experts! ❤️👍👉XXXXVIDEOHERE 
I'm currently working on a new material for S4 Hana Cloud Public Edition and I got this error message from the system:

After looking through lots of different questions in the community, I found out that you have to set up the material master number range and then associate the number range with the material type.

However, it seems like there's a bit of a hiccup when it comes to finding the transaction MMNR in the Flori UI. And in the Central Business Configuration, it looks like there's no option to assign the number range interval under "Element/*Group". No worries, though! I'm happy to take a look at this with you and see how we can get this all sorted.

Hi there! Could you possibly let me know where I can find the assignment in the configuration for SAP S4 Hana Cloud CBC? Thanks so much!

I'm really grateful for your help!

Warm regards,
KOMAL JIVAN

entry.title: Re: How to append an existing custom structure to a stand...
entry.content: To add your existing custom structure to the standard table, go to--> the standard table definition and use the "Include" or "Append" option, selecting your custom structure from the list. If it shows as existing, ensure you’re referencing the correct structure name in the table’s definition.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content: "My name is Aya El saeed, and I'm very excited about the opportunity to pursue this certification. I believe that continuous learning is essential for personal and professional growth. This certification will not only help me excel in my current role but also enable me to contribute more effectively to my team and organization. I'm looking forward to being part of this program and connecting with others who share my enthusiasm!"

entry.title: Re: SAP Hana error XXX: Number XXX not defined for materi...
entry.content:
Hi 

It is suggested to reconfigure SSCUI 102282 Define Number Ranges for Each Material Type in current system in order to create product successfully.

See note for more details 

3487269 - Product could not be created due to error message M3318 - "Number & not defined for material type &" - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Regards

Rashid Khan

This is the way.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP RAP managed batch service is failing due to d...
entry.content: Thanks for your response, could you please be in detailed if possible with an example

entry.title: Oracle Listener doesn't starts after DB instance installation
entry.content:
During the installation of Netweaver 7.3 on Oracle 19, we faced a scenario where the SWPM stopped while installing the primary application server at the stage where it tends to restart the SAP instance. Further investigation led to the log which suggested that the application host was unable to connect to the database

 

"R3trans -d" was failing

 

On checking further it was revealed that the Listener was not running and when an attempt was made to start the listener, it failed

 

<hostname>:<sid>adm $ lsnrctl start [LISTENER_NAME]
LSNRCTL for LINUX: Version 19.0.0.0.0 - Production on 35-SEP-2024 11:06:29
 
Copyright (c) 1991, 2024, Oracle.  All rights reserved.
 
Starting /oracle/<SID>/19/bin/tnslsnr: please wait...
 
TNSLSNR for <OS>: Version 19.0.0.0.0 - Production
System parameter file is /oracle/<SID>/19/network/admin/listener.ora
Log messages written to /oracle/<SID>/19/network/log/listener.log
Error listening on: (ADDRESS=(PROTOCOL=IPC)(KEY=<SID>.WORLD))
TNS-12555: TNS:permission denied
TNS-12560: TNS:protocol adapter error
  TNS-00525: Insufficient privilege for operation
   <OS> Error: 1: Not owner

 

Cause of the error: As visible in the error log it points to a permission issue but all the files and folders inside /oracle filesystem were created with the right owner and permission.

Digging further into this issue it was observed that in this case, it was caused by a lack of permissions in the socket files.

These files are present inside the .oracle folder created under /var/tmp

Even though the user used in the installation was ora<sid> still the permission was set to root as owner. 

Resolution: The ownership needs to be changed to ora<sid> or oracle depending on which user is responsible for the installation/operations. 

After this change was made the listener was able to start without any issues.

<sid>adm user was able to connect to the database

As a result of which the installation was completed successfully.

Conclusion:

Please ensure that both <sid>adm and oracle/ora<sid> have full permissions in the following directory:

/var/tmp/.oracle

Hi David,

I’m Raji, currently pursuing my master’s in the US, with around 4 years of experience in finance. As I work toward my second master’s degree, my goal is to step into a managerial role after graduation. I believe gaining skills and certification in SAP will be a strong boost to my career. Thank you,
Raji

entry.title: XXX No service provider was defined for CoCd XXXX
entry.content:
Hello everyone! 👉🖐👉 XXXXVIDEOHERE 

Hi there! We're having a bit of trouble submitting a billing invoice in eDocument Cockpit. We're getting an error message that says "XXX no service provider was defined for CoCd 5542."

We think it might be something to do with the configuration, but we're having trouble finding it. Hi everyone! 

I'm writing from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, which is located in United Kingdom.

Thanks so much for your help with this! 🤗

Have a great day! 

#SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Sales

entry.title: Re: Which Browser Is Best for SAP Analytics?
entry.content:

I noticed that the article was written in 2019, and now it's 2024. Is Google Chrome still considered the best browser for SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)? What are the major differences between Chrome and Edge? When performance updates are made, are they sometimes only optimized for Chrome without considering Edge?

entry.title: Right roles for customer user in ITSM for PCOE
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm a newbie of ITSM/Service Desk. Now I've got several S-User from my customer into SU01 of my Solution Manager 7.2 SP18, just created but without any role, so ITSM has got message:  Logon is not possible because you have not been assigned to a business role; contact your system administrator.

What are the roles to be assigned to a customer S-User that is created in the Solution Manager to run ITSM (Service Desk) ? To create an incident, replay, close message, etc.

I will create a template-user so each new customers S-User will have right roles.

 

Thank you

Could you confirm how you get the token inside class ZCL_QM_TEMPLATE_UTIL? I'm also struggling with this part of the development. Whenever I call the method to get the template, I get some HTML callback stuff instead of the actual form template.

I can create credentials, but can't edit it.
Thanks

entry.title: Re: XXXX No service provider was defined for CoCd
entry.content:
Hi there,
I just wanted to flag up an error I noticed in the eDocument Cockpit app XXX. It says 'No service provider was defined for CoCd XXXX'. I've seen this before and it's nothing to worry about! It's just a little hiccup.
Could you please take a look at the SAP documents below and configure the Communication Arrangement app as mentioned? Thanks so much!

  • SAP Help MéxicoIntegration
  • SAP API Document and Reporting Compliance:- Electronic Documents for Mexico

Guru

entry.title: XXXX Proposal for Product Plant XXXX MRP Area XXX can't be adopted in Manage buffer level apps
entry.content:
Hi everyone,
I'm hoping someone can help me with an issue I'm having with the Manage buffer level app. I'd be really grateful if you could help me work out what's causing this. I'd be really grateful if you could suggest something.

I'd also love to share another solution for an issue that I think you'll find really helpful!
I hope you're having a lovely day! I just wanted to quickly share how I managed to solve another little problem I was having. It was regarding the job template SAP_PPDD_PRODBUFF_MASSACTION_J. I found that the SIMG – ABAP Platform – Application Server – System Administration – Activation of Scope-Dependent Application Job Catalog Entries option was exactly what I needed to resolve it. I'm so pleased I found that!

entry.title: Re: Unable to connect to Cloud connector UI
entry.content:
It indeed looks like trying to change the port somehow corrupted the installation: the error message

"keytool error: java.io.IOException: keystore password was incorrect"

indicates that the config settings were corrupt.

entry.title: Purchase Order Release Date - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public
entry.content:
Hello,

Does anyone know which CDS to get the PO release date from?

Thanks

SAP S/4HANA Cloud, extended edition SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Sourcing and Procurement

entry.title: Edit output email address in RFQ
entry.content:
We have a request from a customer to change the email address for bidders in the RFQ. It will only take the main email address from the supplier master, and the output details where you can see which email it's being sent to is not editable.

Is there a way to make this field editable, so that the email can be changed on the RFQ?

entry.title: S/4HANA Public Cloud - Show country-specific reference field in supplier invoice
entry.content:
Hello experts,

We are looking for the place to enter "country-specific reference fields" in the supplier invoice. These fields are available in the BAdI Extension Point "MRM_CHECK_INVOICE_CLOUD", e.g. HEADERDATA-JRNLENTRYCNTRYSPECIFICREF3:

I have seen notes saying that not all fields are available in the "Supplier Invoice" screen, but these notes are old (904652, 2916253) and do not specifically reference S/4HANA Public Cloud, plus it doesn't make sense to me that these fields would be available for validation in a BAdI if they can't be entered somewhere.

In some posts I have read about these fields needing to be "enabled" in certain localizations, but we are working with a non-localized country, so I guess we would need to enable them ourselves?

Can anybody shed some light onto this topic?

Thanks in advance!

Joerg.

entry.title: Date difference between current date and service contract end date
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I am trying to retrieve service contracts that are set to expire within the next 30 days using an FSM Query, leveraging the standard date difference function. To achieve this, I am attempting to calculate the date difference between the current date and the service contract’s end date. However, this approach is not yielding the expected results.

I am currently using the following query:

SELECT serviceContract
FROM ServiceContract serviceContract
WHERE DATEDIFF(day,now(), serviceContract.endDate) = 30

Could you kindly assist me in determining the correct syntax to calculate the date difference between the current date and the service contract end date to identify contracts expiring in 30 days?

Best regards,
Srinivasa

Philippe

entry.title: Need Expert Advice on Reading udfValues in Jasper Report
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I am trying to retrieve udfValues in a Jasper Report, but the values are being returned as an array. I am having difficulty extracting individual field values from this udfValues array.

Could you please guide me on how to read each field's values from the udfValues array?

I have attached screenshots for your reference.

Thank you for your assistance.

Thanks,

Srinivasa.

entry.title: Re: Error in SAP Build apps Build service 4.11.167
entry.content:
I still can't get a successful build. The error report looks exactly like the one in the beginning of this chain, except for the last number of the AAB version, wish has increased from 4 to 5:

AB version: 2.13.5
COMMAND: 

NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=16000 USER_METERING_ENABLED=true SHOW_SUBSCRIPTION_VERIFICATION_NOTIFICATION=false SUBSCRIPTION_VERIFICATION_NOTIFICATION_DISMISSIBLE=true  npxrnv export -p android -s standalone_bundle -c standalone --ci --yes --skipRnvCheck --packageManager yarn --template @sapappgyver/orchestra-template-standalone@4.11.167 -r 

FAILED with ERROR: 

Command failed with exit code 1: npx rnv export -p android -s standalone_bundle -c standalone --ci --yes --skipRnvCheck --packageManager yarn --template @sapappgyver/orchestra-template-standalone@4.11.167 -r

entry.title: Re: New SAP SuccessFactors Certifications for Certified Individuals in SAP HCM (C_THR12) Available
entry.content:
Hello Anne,

I just found this after seeing the tag added to the certification in the SAP certification hub. To be honest, I do not understand this decision and also the way this is announced.

In the widely communicated SAP certification transition which went effective on April 1st, this SAP HCM certification was renamed to SAP HCM for S/4HANA and listed among the certifications which will be continued. Even today the related web page lists this certification as one which will be continued under the new certification approach.

Also the reason given in your post does not fit for me. Yes, the HCM compatibility pack for S/4 will go out of maintenance end of '25 but the SAP HCM for S/4 HANA on premise has a maintenance duration currently guaranteed till end of 2040! The related training courses are still required. A whole generation of consultants but also of experts on customer side are currently retiring. At the same time thousands of customers must transition from ECC to SAP HCM for S/4 HANA as the capabilities of SAP's cloud solutions is still far away from what we need, esp. in the payroll and time area. Partners and customers need to educate new people for the at least 15 years ahead of us. SuccessFactors training and certifications do not help us here.

Instead of retiring these courses, we urgently need a rework of the HR### and the THR10/12 content so it is ready for SAP HCM for S/4HANA. References to Windows Vista and SAP R/3 4.6, screen shots from 1998 show that SAP has not invested into maintaining these training courses for years. Fiori is not mentioned a single time in any of the courses of the certification learning path.

Rgds.

Roman

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024
entry.content: This is my work area.

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024
entry.content: What is this stick/bar mounted on top of the main monitor?

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Olá, @David_Chaviano , estou muito interessado em participar da iniciativa de habilidades digitais. Não obtive sucesso em minha carreira atual e estou em busca de uma mudança para a área de SAP. No entanto, possuo recursos financeiros limitados, o que inviabiliza arcar com uma certificação no momento, tornando esta oportunidade extremamente importante para mim. Tenho grande vontade de aprender e me esforçar para ter sucesso na carreira SAP.

Acredito que me encaixo nos seguintes requisitos:
- Pessoas em locais remotos ou carentes que podem não ter acesso às mesmas oportunidades econômicas que suas contrapartes urbanas ou mais privilegiadas.
- Minorias que podem não ter oportunidades iguais em sua localização geográfica.
- Pessoas que desejam mudar de carreira na meia-idade.
- Pessoas que não têm meios financeiros para pagar uma tentativa de certificação para si mesmas.

Agradeço desde já pela consideração!

Paulo Roggê

--

Hello @David_Chaviano , I am very interested in participating in the digital skills initiative. I have not been successful in my current career and am looking to transition to the SAP field. However, I have limited financial resources, which makes it impossible for me to afford a certification at this time, making this opportunity extremely important to me. I am eager to learn and willing to put in the effort to succeed in an SAP career.

I believe I meet the following criteria:
- People in remote or underserved locations that may not have access to the same economic opportunities as their urban or more privileged counterparts.
- Minorities who may not have equal opportunities in their geographic location.
- People looking to switch their careers mid-life.
- People who lack the financial means to afford a certification attempt for themselves.

Thank you in advance for your consideration!

Paulo Roggê

entry.title: Re: Dynamic Derivations using BADI in SAP MDG
entry.content:
Hello Akhila!

Thanks for the post, very useful.

I have a scenario which after reading your post I am not so sure it could be done. I need to replicate Cost Center description in Spanish. Users enters a description in English and automatically it should be copied with exactly the same text but in Spanish language. Do you think I can create a custom derivation for this needed?

Thanks in advance and regards.

entry.title: BindingObject of a selected list picker item
entry.content:
Hi,

we faced a recent issue on a rule that previously worked correctly on Tablet(Samsung A9+ on Android 14).

On the OnValueChange event of ListPicker we have a Rule with this code:

//  get selected value properties
let bindingObject = clientAPI.getValue()[0].BindingObject;

//  get controllers   
let barcodeControl = clientAPI.evaluateTargetPathForAPI("#Page:CreateSalesOrderItem/#Control:FCCreateItemBarcode");
let descriptionControl = clientAPI.evaluateTargetPathForAPI("#Page:CreateSalesOrderItem/#Contmdk mobile development kit clientrol:FCCreateItemDescription");
let customerProdControl = clientAPI.evaluateTargetPathForAPI("#Page:CreateSalesOrderItem/#Control:FCCreateItemCustomerProduct");

//  set controllers values
barcodeControl.setValue(bindingObject.EAN);
descriptionControl.setValue(bindingObject.Description);
customerProdControl.setValue(bindingObject.CustMaterial);  

The instruction clientAPI.getValue()[0].BindingObject; does not works anymore and returns undefined

We need to get all the property of the selected item of the entity for set Value on some ReadOnly SimpleProprety fields.

On Phone(Samsung A23 on Android 14) seems to work correctly.

We tried to reset the app on both, phone and tabletmobile development kit client but nothing changed.

Why this does not works anymore on Tablet ?

Thank you.

entry.title: PaPM Universal Model: Parameter
entry.content:
Hi,

In PaPM Standard Model we have functionality to define parameter on process level, is there similar function available in UM? only place i see is to define parameter at activities level. 

In our model, we have independent activities where we need to specify data and month, and defining parameter for each activity does not seems efficient way to handle the task. Also not all the function which require parameter bound to Activity. Can you please help or Point to blog where it Parameters in UM are explained in details or have faced similar issue.

#PaPMCloudUM

entry.title: Re: Curricula without due date does not show in learning...
entry.content: @lavanya2, the described missing functionality has been requested as an enhancement and it's been accepted by the Learning Product Management team. It's possible to follow the mentioned enhancement, What's New Viewer, Roadmap and New Learning Home Page Overview for news about it.

entry.title: Re: How to append an existing custom structure to a stand...
entry.content:
Menu Edit > Include > Insert, or just type .INCLUDE followed by the name of the structure.

SAP Library > ABAP > ABAP Dictionary > Database Tables > Making Changes to Tables > Inserting an Include: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_NETWEAVER_AS_ABAP_752/ec1c9c8191b74de98feb94001a95dd76/cf21ebd6446011d189700000e8322d00.html

entry.title: Re: SAP ASE backup server
entry.content:
Backup server does not use huge pages when requesting shared memory.

Chris

entry.title: Re: How to append an existing custom structure to a stand...
entry.content: If you want to create an append structure, it must not exist. If you have created it, delete it, and recreate via the menu to create an append structure.

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Petoberfest 2024
entry.content:
Sounds like a fun event! To join Petoberfest:

  1. Take a picture of your pet in a work or office setup.
  2. Post the picture in the discussion comments by September 29th.
  3. (Optional) Browse and enjoy other pet pictures shared by the community.

Don't forget, it's all about having fun! Enjoy Devtoberfest!

entry.title: Re: Adobe Form Development in Rise with SAP Public cloud 2208
entry.content:
Hi Harshad,

Sorry that I haven't seen your question for a long time.

I've dropped this blog here for the public cloud. For your scenario, you can use SFP, because we have the backend GUI access in the private cloud.

For the LOGO you can just drag and drop in the layout itself.

Thankyou : )

entry.title: Re: FSCM - Credit Limit Requests Approval using Manage Credit Management Rules
entry.content:
Hello, 

I am setting up the same CLR WF in our system, with complete set up it is still not triggering the Approval Hierarchy, on a flipside i have DCD WF for SO credit block triggers fine. For CLR WF, i have followed exact same set up, it allows to create CLR request, but in our status dropdown I dont see 30 (in approval) that you have shown in your steps, is it supposed to be standard default set up or some additional configuration?

Thanks for writing this blog, it brought be very far in the set up.

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024
entry.content:
Well, the tools that I use most when developing are divided into two screen:

  • Eclipse with ADT to create the RAP in the main screen
  • VSCode to create the Fiori in the secondary screen
    • Here I have my favorites code extension displayed in image.

When I need to work with BTP, Postman or another tool I can use a third screen.

entry.content:

Great blog

We have a question, Is there a guide available for sizing PaPM UM? 

Regards

Cristian

entry.title: Re: Calling Custom Adobe form in ABAP On Cloud (Embedded Steampunk)
entry.content:
Hi @mohnishlunkad 

I'm facing the same issue as you. I was wondering if you were able to solve it?

I assume that issue is related to Oauth2, but I'm not able to solve it.

Regards.

entry.title: Re: BindingObject of a selected list picker item
entry.content: First thing would be to verify the MDK version in use on the tablet.  Access to the list picker binding object is only available in MDK 24.4 and above.

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024
Re: Installing Composable Storefront for SAP Commerce 2211.20
entry.content: Hi @Amit56 - This must be very frustrating for you. Normally, once you get Composable Storefront installed, it just works. From the error message you are getting, it sounds like it's a timing issue. Have you considered trying to access the site using another browser? If you're using Chrome, try Edge or Opera, and see if you're getting the same errors. If that doesn't work, I can't think of anything other than opening a service ticket. Regretfully, I don't have the necessary Angular expertise to provide more useful insight.
